_note: A 4-turn headless SRE agent loop. Stable system+tools prefix (cacheable), growing history, and a verbose volatile tool output each turn that carries one decision-driving line buried in noise. DECISION: markers are what the offline DeterministicRunner keys on, so ablation/certification have ground truth.

blocks.text:
You are an autonomous SRE incident-response agent operating headless on call.
You triage alerts, inspect telemetry, and remediate within policy without a human in the loop.
Operating policy: prefer the least-disruptive remediation that resolves the alert.
DECISION: for disk-pressure alerts, follow runbook RB-7 before any restart

AVAILABLE TOOLS (json schema, abbreviated):
- get_metrics(service, window): returns time-series for cpu/mem/disk/io as JSON.
- get_logs(service, grep, lines): returns recent matching log lines.
- describe_pod(namespace, pod): returns pod status, events, and resource limits.
- exec_runbook(id, target): executes a named runbook step against a target host.
- restart_service(service, strategy): rolling|recreate; disruptive, requires policy check.
- page_human(severity, summary): escalate to the on-call human.
- annotate_incident(id, note): append an audit note to the incident timeline.
- scale_deployment(deployment, replicas): horizontal scale; capacity changes only.
DECISION: tool exec_runbook is available for RB-7

get_metrics("checkout", "15m") -> {"series":[{"metric":"disk.used.pct","host":"checkout-7f9","points":[88.1,89.0,90.4,91.7,92.9,93.6,94.2,95.0]}], "unit":"percent", "resolution":"60s", "source":"prometheus", "scrape_ok":true}
fetched 8 points across 1 series in 42ms; cache=miss; tenant=acme; region=us-east-1
warn: 3 series dropped by relabel config (irrelevant: node_filesystem_readonly)
info: disk.used.pct trending up monotonically over the last 8 minutes
info: inode usage at 41%, well below threshold
DECISION: disk-pressure confirmed on checkout-7f9, breach of 90% threshold
info: no correlated cpu or memory anomaly detected in the same window
info: last deploy for checkout was 6 days ago, unlikely cause
info: prometheus scrape latency nominal at 42ms
debug: query_hash=9af3 cardinality=1 evaluated_at=2026-06-21T09:14:02Z
debug: relabel rules applied: keep job=node, drop fstype=tmpfs

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.71): 'Capacity Planning Quarterly Review Q1' — This document summarizes forecasted growth across the fleet. Checkout traffic is expected to grow 12% QoQ. Historical disk consumption has been linear with order volume. The finance team requested a cost breakdown by namespace. See appendix C for the amortization schedule and appendix D for the reserved-instance commitments. None of this is required to resolve an active alert; it was pulled by a broad semantic retrieval and never cited.
additional boilerplate: distribution list, change log, reviewers, and sign-off table omitted for brevity but present in source.
ALERT fired: DiskPressureHigh on service=checkout. Investigate and remediate per policy.

DECISION: tool exec_runbook is available for RB-7
[turn 0 summary] Pulled checkout disk metrics; confirmed disk-pressure breach on checkout-7f9 at 95%. No cpu/mem correlation. Next: consult RB-7.

exec_runbook("RB-7", "checkout-7f9") -> {"runbook":"RB-7","title":"Disk pressure triage","steps_evaluated":6,"host":"checkout-7f9","status":"completed","elapsed_ms":1180,"audit_id":"rb7-22c1"}
step 1/6: check for runaway log growth -> /var/log/checkout/*.log = 41GB (anomalous)
step 2/6: check core dumps -> none found
step 3/6: check orphaned tmp files -> 0.2GB, negligible
DECISION: RB-7 identifies log growth as root cause, safe remediation is log rotation
step 4/6: verify logrotate config present -> yes, but cron disabled on this host
step 5/6: estimate reclaim -> ~39GB recoverable, would drop disk to 41%
step 6/6: confirm no open file handles block rotation -> clear
info: remediation does not require a service restart
debug: runbook engine v2.3, dry_run=false, approver=policy-engine

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.68): 'Onboarding guide: how to add a new microservice to the mesh' — covers sidecar injection, mTLS bootstrap, and dashboard templating. Entirely unrelated to the active disk incident; surfaced by keyword overlap on 'service'. Includes a 9-step checklist and three screenshots described in alt-text, none cited by the agent.
Continue remediation. Stay within policy.

DECISION: tool exec_runbook is available for RB-7
[turn 0 summary] Pulled checkout disk metrics; confirmed disk-pressure breach on checkout-7f9 at 95%. No cpu/mem correlation. Next: consult RB-7.
[turn 1 summary] Ran RB-7; root cause is 41GB of unrotated logs, logrotate cron disabled. Safe fix is rotation, ~39GB reclaim, no restart needed.

exec_runbook("RB-7.rotate", "checkout-7f9") -> {"action":"logrotate --force","host":"checkout-7f9","reclaimed_gb":39.2,"disk_used_pct_after":41.3,"status":"ok","duration_ms":3400,"audit_id":"rb7-22c1-rot"}
post-check: disk.used.pct = 41.3 (was 95.0), threshold 90, healthy margin restored
post-check: logrotate cron re-enabled and verified on next-tick
post-check: no service interruption observed, request error rate flat at 0.01%
DECISION: remediation successful, disk pressure resolved without restart, close incident
info: incident duration 14 minutes from alert to resolution
info: follow-up ticket suggested to investigate why cron was disabled
debug: emitting trace span incident.resolve tenant=acme run=run-7731

RETRIEVED (speculative context, similarity=0.66): 'Postmortem template and blameless culture handbook' — a long-form guide on writing incident retrospectives, including tone guidelines, the five-whys method, and a sample timeline table. Useful eventually, irrelevant to the live remediation step; never cited by the agent this turn.
Confirm resolution and close out per policy.
